text.title: langchain-agents-architectures Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for langchain-agents-architectures as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: ReAct tool hallucination: model calls tools not in the schema or calls them with invalid argument types
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Use `handle_tool_error=True` and Pydantic-validated inputs. `tool_choice="auto"` does not guarantee valid calls. The model may still invent tool names or pass wrong types. Always validate and set a meaningful error response.

failure-mode.description: Plan-and-Execute plan staleness: the upfront plan does not account for intermediate tool results
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: A failed step 2 leaves steps 3-N invalid. The plan is static but execution is dynamic. Add a re-plan gate after each step that checks whether the remaining plan is still valid. If a step fails or returns unexpected results, trigger a re-planning phase to adjust the remaining steps.

failure-mode.description: LATS cost: tree search with 3 candidates × 5 evaluation rounds = 15+ LLM calls per problem
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Cost scales poorly with search depth. Each candidate must be generated and scored; deeper trees explode in cost. Use Haiku for scoring and Sonnet for expansion to reduce cost by 70%. In production, limit LATS to 2 branches with 3 depth maximum.

failure-mode.description: Tool error propagation: a single tool failure raises an exception that aborts the agent run
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Unless `handle_tool_error=True` is set, the run fails and the entire agent workflow stops. Always set this flag. Also provide a meaningful error message so the agent understands what went wrong and can retry with different inputs.

failure-mode.description: Context window growth: long ReAct runs fill the context window
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: A 20-step ReAct run with verbose tool outputs can consume 50K+ tokens. LangChain does not auto-truncate by default. Truncate tool outputs to 2K characters maximum before appending to state. Better: extract only the relevant excerpt from the tool output and discard the rest.

failure-mode.description: LATS scoring reliability: LLM-based scoring of candidate thoughts has low inter-rater reliability
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The "best path" selected may not be objectively best. Use scoring as a heuristic, not ground truth.

failure-mode.description: LangGraph schema rigidity: changing `PlanExecuteState` mid-project requires migrating checkpointed state
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Plan the schema carefully upfront. Schema changes are difficult mid-project.
